translation: Though deeply pierced with that Śakti (spear) Garuḍa did not move at all like an elephant struck with a garland, in that fight with Naraka.

translation: Thereupon, taking up his bow Naraka discharged a shower of arrows on Kṛṣṇa, but Keśava cut them all with his shafts.

devanagari: गिरिदुर्गैः शस्त्रदुर्गैः जलाग्न्यनिलदुर्गमम् । मुरपाशायुतैर्घोरैः दृढैः सर्वत आवृतम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: giridurgaiḥ śastradurgaiḥ jalāgnyaniladurgamam murapāśāyutairghoraiḥ dṛḍhaiḥ sarvata āvṛtam
translation: Noticing that Naraka had properly set an ardha-candra arrow (with semi-circular head) and was about to discharge by pulling the bowstring, Kṛṣṇa cut down his bow.

devanagari: गदया निर्बिभेदाद्रीन् शस्त्रदुर्गाणि सायकैः । चक्रेणाग्निं जलं वायुं मुरपाशांस्तथासिना ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: gadayā nirbibhedādrīn śastradurgāṇi sāyakaiḥ cakreṇāgniṃ jalaṃ vāyuṃ murapāśāṃstathāsinā

translation: Then the son of the mother Earth picked up his heavy mace of hard iron, but Kṛṣṇa cut it with an arrow called Kṣurapra (an arrow with shoe-shaped sharp head).

devanagari: शङ्खनादेन यन्त्राणि हृदयानि मनस्विनाम् । प्राकारं गदया गुर्व्या निर्बिभेद गदाधरः ॥ ५ ॥

translation: After this. Naraka, the vanquisher of hostile forces hurled his sharp-edged axe at Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa) but he cut it down with his sword.

devanagari: पाञ्चजन्यध्वनिं श्रुत्वा युगान्तशनिभीषणम् । मुरः शयान उत्तस्थौ दैत्यः पञ्चशिरा जलात् ॥ ६ ॥

translation: Then he threw at Kṛṣṇa seventy-five Tomaras (javelins) and Naraka, the destroyer of hostile heroes, roared like a lion.

devanagari: त्रिशूलमुद्यम्य सुदुर्निरीक्षणो । युगान्तसूर्यानलरोचिरुल्बणः । ग्रसंस्त्रिलोकीमिव पञ्चभिर्मुखैः । अभ्यद्रवत्तार्क्ष्यसुतं यथोरगः ॥ ७ ॥

translation: Kṛṣṇa simultaneously cut down all the javelins with one arrow. It was simply a miracle, O Prominent Kuru.

devanagari: आविध्य शूलं तरसा गरुत्मते । निरस्य वक्त्रैर्व्यनदत् स पञ्चभिः । स रोदसी सर्वदिशोऽन्तरं महान् । आपूरयन् अण्डकटाहमावृणोत् ॥ ८ ॥

translation: Brandishing the trident with great velocity, he hurled it with all his might at Garuḍa and roared with all his five mouths. The loud roar filled the earth and the heaven, all the directions and the firmament in fact all the cavity of the Brahmāṇḍa (universe).

devanagari: तदापतद् वै त्रिशिखं गरुत्मते । हरिः शराभ्यां अभिनत्त्रिधौजसा । मुखेषु तं चापि शरैरताडयत् । तस्मै गदां सोऽपि रुषा व्यमुञ्चत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadāpatad vai triśikhaṃ garutmate hariḥ śarābhyāṃ abhinattridhaujasā mukheṣu taṃ cāpi śarairatāḍayat tasmai gadāṃ so

translation: Thereupon powerfully hitting two arrows at the trident as it was dashing against Garuḍa, He cut it into three and stuffed the demon’s mouths with arrows. The demon (on his part) vehemently hurled his mace at Kṛṣṇa in that battle.

devanagari: तामापतन्तीं गदया गदां मृधे । गदाग्रजो निर्बिभिदे सहस्रधा । उद्यम्य बाहूनभिधावतोऽजितः । शिरांसि चक्रेण जहार लीलया ॥ १० ॥

translation: In that battle, the invincible elder brother of Gada (i.e. Śrī Kṛṣṇa) shattered, with his mace, the on-rushing mace to a thousand pieces. And as the demon was rusing [rushing]? at him with his arms raised-up, he easily severed off his heads with his discus.

devanagari: व्यसुः पपाताम्भसि कृत्तशीर्षो । निकृत्तशृङ्गोऽद्रिरिवेन्द्रतेजसा । तस्यात्मजाः सप्त पितुर्वधातुराः । प्रतिक्रियामर्षजुषः समुद्यताः ॥ १...

translation: With his heads dissevered, the demon fell lifeless into the water like a mountain with its peak cut off by the mighty Indra. Grieved at their father’s death, his seven sons, enraged and impatient to avenge it, rushed at Kṛṣṇa fully prepared[5].
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī’s Text differs:
11(A). When the demon Mura was killed, a shower of flowers expressing joy at the event, fell on the head of Viṣṇu (i.e. Kṛṣṇa)

devanagari: ताम्रोऽन्तरिक्षः श्रवणो विभावसुः । वसुर्नभस्वानरुणश्च सप्तमः । पीठं पुरस्कृत्य चमूपतिं मृधे । भौमप्रयुक्ता निरग धृतायुधाः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmro vasurnabhasvānaruṇaśca saptamaḥ pīṭhaṃ puraskṛtya camūpatiṃ mṛdhe bhaumaprayuktā niraga dhṛtāyudhāḥ
translation: Directed by Naraka, the son of the Earth, the seven sons of Mura viz. Tāmra, Antarīkṣa, Śravaṇa, Vibhāvasu, Vasu, Nabhasvān and the seventh Aruṇa, with their commander Pītha at their head, sallied forth to the battlefield, fully equipped with arms.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī’s Reading:
2.1. Directed by Naraka and riding their chariots drawn by bulls, those warriors (sons of Mura) sallied forth from their homes. Overwhelmed with rage they discharged from their well-strung bow, weapons, like darts, ṛṣṭis (double edged weapons) and javelins at the unconquered Lord).

devanagari: प्रायुञ्जतासाद्य शरानसीन् गदाः । शक्त्यृष्टिशूलान्यजिते रुषोल्बणाः । तच्छस्त्रकूटं भगवान् स्वमार्गणैः । अमोघवीर्यस्तिलशश्चकर्त ह ॥ १३ ...
english_devnagari: prāyuñjatāsādya śarānasīn gadāḥ śaktyṛṣṭiśūlānyajite ruṣolbaṇāḥ tacchastrakūṭaṃ bhagavān svamārgaṇaiḥ amoghavīryastilaśaścakarta ha

translation: The remaining warriors then immediately sped to the great capital (Prāgjyotiṣapura) and reported to Naraka that all sons of Mura were killed.

devanagari: तान्पीठमुख्याननयद् यमक्षयं । निकृत्तशीर्षोरुभुजाङ्घ्रिवर्मणः । स्वानीकपानच्युतचक्रसायकैः । तथा निरस्तान् नरको धरासुतः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tānpīṭhamukhyānanayad yamakṣayaṃ nikṛttaśīrṣorubhujāṅghrivarmaṇaḥ svānīkapānacyutacakrasāyakaiḥ tathā nirastān narako dharāsutaḥ
translation: Surrounded with thousands of chariots and elephants and accompanied by a vast army, Naraka arrived at the battlefield.

devanagari: निरीक्ष्य दुर्मर्षण आस्रवन्मदैः । गजैः पयोधिप्रभवैर्निराक्रमात् । दृष्ट्वा सभार्यं गरुडोपरि स्थितं । सूर्योपरिष्टात् सतडिद्घनं यथा । क...
english_devnagari: nirīkṣya durmarṣaṇa āsravanmadaiḥ gajaiḥ payodhiprabhavairnirākramāt dṛṣṭvā sabhāryaṃ garuḍopari sthitaṃ sūryopariṣṭāt sataḍidghanaṃ yathā kṛṣṇaṃ sa tasmai vyasṛjacchataghnīṃ yodhāśca sarve yugapat ca vivyadhuḥ

translation: It was a miracle when the volley of innumerable arrows discharged by Naraka were simultaneously splintered with his shafts smilingly despatched by the Lord.

translation: [8] With his sharp pointed arrows equipped with variegated feathers, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the elder brother of Gada, mowed down the army of Naraka cutting down the arms, thighs, necks and trunks of the soldiers simultaneously killing the horses and elephants.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Padaratnāvalī’s wording of the 1st half differs:
Then with volleys of sharp arrows decked with variegated feathers discharged at Naraka’s army, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, etc.

devanagari: यानि योधैः प्रयुक्तानि शस्त्रास्त्राणि कुरूद्वह । हरिस्तान्यच्छिनत् तीक्ष्णैः शरैरेकैक शस्त्रीभिः ॥ १७ ॥

translation: (What a wonderful feat) O scion of the Kuru race! Hari cut down with three sharp arrows each of the weapons and missiles discharged (at Hari) by the hostile soldiers.

devanagari: उह्यमानः सुपर्णेन पक्षाभ्यां निघ्नता गजान् । गुरुत्मता हन्यमानाः तुण्डपक्षनखैर्गजाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: uhyamānaḥ suparṇena pakṣābhyāṃ nighnatā gajān gurutmatā hanyamānāḥ tuṇḍapakṣanakhairgajāḥ

translation: He was borne by Garuḍa who (on his part) struck down elephants with his wings, killing them with his bill, claws and wings.

devanagari: पुरमेवाविशन्नार्ता नरको युध्ययुध्यत । दृष्ट्वा विद्रावितं सैन्यं गरुडेनार्दितं स्वकं ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: puramevāviśannārtā narako yudhyayudhyata dṛṣṭvā vidrāvitaṃ sainyaṃ garuḍenārditaṃ svakaṃ

translation: Distressed by Garuḍa’s attacks they retreated into the city. Seeing his army killed and routed by Garuḍa, Naraka continued to fight single-handed.

devanagari: तं भौमः प्राहरच्छक्त्या वज्रः प्रतिहतो यतः । नाकम्पत तया विद्धो मालाहत इव द्विपः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ bhaumaḥ prāharacchaktyā vajraḥ pratihato yataḥ nākampata tayā viddho mālāhata iva dvipaḥ

translation: Kṛṣṇa simultaneously cut down all the javelins with one arrow. It was simply a miracle, O Prominent Kuru.

devanagari: शूलं भौमोऽच्युतं हन्तुं आददे वितथोद्यमः । तद्विसर्गात् पूर्वमेव नरकस्य शिरो हरिः । अपाहरद् गजस्थस्य चक्रेण क्षुरनेमिना ॥ २१ ॥

translation: His attempt having been thus frustrated, Naraka, the son of the Earth goddess, took up his spear to strike at Kṛṣṇa, but before he could hurl it, Hari, with his discus (Sudarśana) of razor-like sharp edge, severed the head of Naraka who was riding on elephant.

devanagari: सकुण्डलं चारुकिरीटभूषणं । बभौ पृथिव्यां पतितं समुज्ज्वलम् । हा हेति साध्वित्यृषयः सुरेश्वरा । माल्यैर्मुकुन्दं विकिरन्त ईडिरे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sakuṇḍalaṃ cārukirīṭabhūṣaṇaṃ babhau pṛthivyāṃ patitaṃ samujjvalam hā heti sādhvityṛṣayaḥ sureśvarā mālyairmukundaṃ vikiranta īḍire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ ir class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ ir class 6 verb]

translation: The head still adorned with ear-rings and a beautiful crown, though lying on the ground was shining, splendidly ‘Alas!’ “Bravo! Well done” applauded the sages, while the lords of gods, showering flowers on Śrī Kṛṣṇa, eulogized him.

devanagari: ततश्च भूः कृष्णमुपेत्य कुण्डले । प्रतप्तजाम्बूनदरत्‍नभास्वरे । सवैजयन्त्या वनमालयार्पयत् । प्राचेतसं छत्रमथो महामणिम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca bhūḥ kṛṣṇamupetya kuṇḍale prataptajāmbūnadarat‍nabhāsvare savaijayantyā vanamālayārpayat prācetasaṃ chatramatho mahāmaṇim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[ablative single from √ ej ], [genitive single from √ ej ]
[nominative single from √ ṛ ], [vocative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]

translation: Thereupon the goddess Earth approached Kṛṣṇa and handed over to him a pair of ear-rings resplendent with jewels and chased in the purest gold (belonging to Aditi), accompanied with the necklace called Vaijayantī[9] and a garland of forest flowers (vanamālā), the umbrella (the royal insignia) of Varuṇa as well as a great jewel (or the peak of mount Mandara called Maṇiparvata—vide verse 2 above).
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Padaratnāvalī takes Vaijayantī as ‘garland of flowers of five colours’ and Vanamālā as ‘a big garland of flowers and leaves reaching the foot,’ but Bhāgavata Candrikā regards both as one : vaijayantyā vana-mālayā saha.

devanagari: अस्तौषीदथ विश्वेशं देवी देववरार्चितम् । प्राञ्जलिः प्रणता राजन् भक्तिप्रवणया धिया ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: astauṣīdatha viśveśaṃ devī devavarārcitam prāñjaliḥ praṇatā rājan bhaktipravaṇayā dhiyā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: The goddess folded her palms, bowed down to him and with her heart full of sincere devotion extolled the Lord of the universe who is adored by gods.

devanagari: भूमिरुवाच । नमस्ते देवदेवेश शङ्खचक्रगदाधर । भक्तेच्छोपात्तरूपाय परमात्मन्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmiruvāca namaste devadeveśa śaṅkhacakragadādhara bhaktecchopāttarūpāya paramātmannamo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Earth-goddess prayed:
Salutations to you, Oh God of gods, Oh wielder of a conch, a discus and a mace! I bow to you, Oh Supreme Soul who have assumed form in compliance with the wishes of your votaries.

devanagari: नमः पङ्कजनाभाय नमः पङ्कजमालिने । नमः पङ्कजनेत्राय नमस्ते पङ्कजाङ्घ्रये ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ paṅkajanābhāya namaḥ paṅkajamāline namaḥ paṅkajanetrāya namaste paṅkajāṅghraye
translation: Hail to You, O God with a lotus in the navel (and are thus the cause—creator—of the universe). I bow to You O wearer of the wreath of lotuses (signifying your glorious renown). Salutations to You, O God with lotus eyes (soothing the torments of the world). Bow unto You, O God with lotus feet (deserving adoration and service by the world).

devanagari: नमो भगवते तुभ्यं वासुदेवाय विष्णवे । पुरुषायादिबीजाय पूर्णबोधाय ते नमः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: namo bhagavate tubhyaṃ vāsudevāya viṣṇave puruṣāyādibījāya pūrṇabodhāya te namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]
[vocative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: Hail to the Lord of (the highest) divine faculties (such as omnipotence etc.), Vāsudeva—the resort of all beings and the All-pervading god Viṣṇu. You are the Supreme Person, present before all the evolutes[10], the Creator (or the Cause) of the Primordial matter (Prakṛti). I bow to you, Perfect Spiritual Wisdom incarnate.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a Śruti text to support this:
pūrvam evāham ihāsam iti tat puruṣaṣya puruṣatvam /

devanagari: अजाय जनयित्रेऽस्य ब्रह्मणेऽनन्तशक्तये । परावरात्मन् भूतात्मन् परमात्मन् नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ajāya janayitre parāvarātman bhūtātman paramātman namo
translation: Hail to you who are yourself unborn but are the Creator of this Universe; who are the Brahman itself of infinite potencies. You are the Inner Controller of the great and the small, the Master of all elements (or created beings), O Supreme Soul.

devanagari: त्वं वै सिसृक्षुरज उत्कटं प्रभो । तमो निरोधाय बिभर्ष्यसंवृतः । स्थानाय सत्त्वं जगतो जगत्पते । कालः प्रधानं पुरुषो भवान् परः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ vai sisṛkṣuraja utkaṭaṃ prabho tamo nirodhāya bibharṣyasaṃvṛtaḥ sthānāya sattvaṃ jagato jagatpate kālaḥ pradhānaṃ puruṣo bhavān paraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: [11] O Lord when desirous cf creating the universe you assume intense form of rajoguṇa (viz. the four-headed god Brahmā); for the dissolution of the world you support the Tamoguṇa (and assume the form of god Rudra). And for the continuance of the world, you assume the sattva (i.e. Viṣṇu form). You are no other than the Time (Kāla), the Primordial matter (Prakṛti) and the supreme Spirit itself—yet you are distinct from them.[12]
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: O omnipresent Lord! When desirous of creation, you do so by entering the body of the four-faced god (Brahmā) characterised by rajo-guṇa. For destruction (of the universe) you enter into the person of Rudra, Kālāgni (Fire burning down the universe) etc. who are dominated by tamas. For protection of the world, you enter into the bodies of Manu and other in whom sattva-guṇa is predominant as well as by incarnating yourself directly. It is due to your presence in Kāla (The Time Spirit), Primordial matter (Prakṛti) and the individual Soul that you are designated as Kāla though distinct from them. Bhāgavata Candrikā quotes:
aitad ātmyam idaṃ sarvam, tat satyam, sa ātmā—Chāndogya 6.8.7 etc.

Footnote [12]:
Subodhinī: emphasizes that God transcends the guṇas.
Padaratnāvalī You are Kāla (the Time-Spirit) which gives effect to the Fate (adṛṣṭa) of beings. You are called Pradhāna as the exalted-most, Supreme Person and are so designated. Or You are puruṣottama and hence called Pradhāna Puruṣa.

devanagari: अहं पयो ज्योतिरथानिलो नभो । मात्राणि देवा मन इन्द्रियाणि । कर्ता महानित्यखिलं चराचरं । त्वय्यद्वितीये भगवन्नयं भ्रमः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ payo jyotirathānilo nabho mātrāṇi devā mana indriyāṇi kartā mahānityakhilaṃ carācaraṃ tvayyadvitīye bhagavannayaṃ bhramaḥ
translation:
You are one without a second, i.e. the earth (and other elements like) water, fire, wind and the sky, the five tanmātras (the subtle elements with their qualities such as sound, taste, touch, form and fragrance), the presiding deities over senses, mind, cognitive and conative senses, ahaṃkāra (Ego, I- ness), Mahat (the Principle of Cosmic Intelligence), the mobile and immobile creation all these are illusions imposed on you.
[Yande’s Bombay edition adds in the footnote (P. 857).]
30(A). When I was lifted by you, O Lord, in your boar incarnation, this son (Naraka) was born of me in consequence of your contact. So you gave that son to me and now he has been struck down by you.

devanagari: तस्यात्मजोऽयं तव पादपङ्कजं । भीतः प्रपन्नार्तिहरोपसादितः । तत् पालयैनं कुरु हस्तपङ्कजं । शिरस्यमुष्याखिलकल्मषापहम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyātmajo bhītaḥ prapannārtiharopasāditaḥ tat pālayainaṃ kuru hastapaṅkajaṃ śirasyamuṣyākhilakalmaṣāpaham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: This (Bhagadatta) is his (Naraka’s) son. He is afraid and hence I have brought him to your feet, as you remove all the miseries of those who are afflicted. Be pleased to protect him by placing on his head your lotus-like hand which washes off all sins and impurities.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति भूम्यर्थितो वाग्भिः भगवान् भक्तिनम्रया । दत्त्वाभयं भौमगृहं प्राविशत् सकलर्द्धिमत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti bhūmyarthito vāgbhiḥ bhagavān bhaktinamrayā dattvābhayaṃ bhaumagṛhaṃ prāviśat sakalarddhimat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When the glorious Lord was entreated in these words by the goddess Earth in devotion and humility, the Lord vouchsafed him protection and entered the palace of Naraka, full of all affluence.
[Padaratnāvalī’s text adds]
32(A). Having installed him (Naraka’s son, Bhagadatta) on his hereditary throne (lit. kingdom) and reappointed his ministers to their respective posts, Hari entered the series of compartments and dwelling units of Naraka’s harem.

devanagari: तत्र राजन्यकन्यानां षट्सहस्राधिकायुतम् । भौमाहृतानां विक्रम्य राजभ्यो ददृशे हरिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra rājanyakanyānāṃ ṣaṭsahasrādhikāyutam bhaumāhṛtānāṃ vikramya rājabhyo dadṛśe hariḥ
translation: Hari found there sixteen thousand (and one hundred—) daughters of kings (Siddhas, gods, asuras etc.) brought as captives after defeating various kings[13].
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā bases his interpretation on the basis of Viṣṇu Purāṇa: 5.29.31 (regarding additional one hundred girls) but the second verse quoted by him—
deva-siddhāsurādīnāṃ ṇṛpāṇāṃ ca janārdana /
hṛtvā hi so'suraḥ kanyā rurodha nijamandire //
Is not traced in the Gītā Press edition.

devanagari: तं प्रविष्टं स्त्रियो वीक्ष्य नरवर्यं विमोहिताः । मनसा वव्रिरेऽभीष्टं पतिं दैवोपसादितम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ praviṣṭaṃ striyo vīkṣya naravaryaṃ vimohitāḥ manasā vavrire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ ], [accusative single from √ vṛ ]

translation: [14] Perceiving the great hero among men enter, the damsels were so fascinated with him that they mentally chose him as their desired-most beloved spouse brought to them by their Destiny.
purport:
Footnote [14]:
Padaratnāvalī’s reading: Seeing him enter, the damsels who were all youthful, well-ornamented and of attractive features were fascinated with the great hero among men.

devanagari: भूयात् पतिरयं मह्यं धाता तदनुमोदताम् । इति सर्वाः पृथक् कृष्णे भावेन हृदयं दधुः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūyāt patirayaṃ mahyaṃ dhātā tadanumodatām iti sarvāḥ pṛthak kṛṣṇe bhāvena hṛdayaṃ dadhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: “May this hero be my husband. May god Brahmā (or the Creator) approve and grant my prayer.” With this devout feeling for him, all of them severally set their hearts on Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: ताः प्राहिणोद् द्वारवतीं सुमृष्टविरजोऽम्बराः । नरयानैर्महाकोशान् रथाश्वान् द्रविणं महत् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāḥ prāhiṇod dvāravatīṃ sumṛṣṭavirajo narayānairmahākośān rathāśvān draviṇaṃ mahat
translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa sent them all to Dvārakā in palanquins after they had taken their bath and put on excellent clean dress[15]. He also despatched along with them valuable treasure (of precious stones etc.) as well as chariots, horses and immense wealth.
purport:
Footnote [15]:
VT. explains that those girls led an austere life, wearing tawny- coloured clothes observing vows and fasts.

devanagari: ऐरावतकुलेभांश्च चतुर्दन्तांस्तरस्विनः । पाण्डुरांश्च चतुःषष्टिं प्रेरयामास केशवः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: airāvatakulebhāṃśca caturdantāṃstarasvinaḥ pāṇḍurāṃśca catuḥṣaṣṭiṃ prerayāmāsa keśavaḥ
translation:
Krṣṇa sent sixty-four, white, four-tusked swift-footed elephants of Airāvata’s pedigree to Dvārakā.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
37(A). He gave to Bhagadatta one elephant, called Supratīka out of them and sent the rest to the shed.

devanagari: गत्वा सुरेन्द्रभवनं दत्त्वादित्यै च कुण्डले । पूजितस्त्रिदशेन्द्रेण सहेन्द्र्याण्या च सप्रियः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: gatvā surendrabhavanaṃ dattvādityai ca kuṇḍale pūjitastridaśendreṇa sahendryāṇyā ca sapriyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Having gone to the palace of Indra and restored to Aditi her two ear-rings, he along with his beloved consort Satyabhāmā was worshipped by the king of gods along with his queen Indrāṇī.

devanagari: चोदितो भार्ययोत्पाट्य पारीजातं गरुत्मति । आरोप्य सेन्द्रान् विबुधान् निर्जित्योपानयत् पुरम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: codito bhāryayotpāṭya pārījātaṃ garutmati āropya sendrān vibudhān nirjityopānayat puram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Importunated by his consort, he uprooted the celestial tree Pārijāta and placed it on (his mount) Garuḍa. After defeating the celestials (who resisted the taking away of Pārijāta), he brought it to his capital Dvārakā.[16]
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Padaratnāvalī,’s text elaborated this episode in two chapters, (ch. 65 and 66) which are translated in the Appendix as chapter 59-1 and 59-II.

devanagari: स्थापितः सत्यभामाया गृहोद्यानोपशोभनः । अन्वगुर्भ्रमराः स्वर्गात् तद् गन्धासवलम्पटाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: sthāpitaḥ satyabhāmāyā gṛhodyānopaśobhanaḥ anvagurbhramarāḥ svargāt tad gandhāsavalampaṭāḥ
translation: It was planted as an (additional) adornment to the garden of Satyabhāmā’s palace. Covetously hankering after its fragrance and intoxicating honey, black bees from heaven followed it.[17]
purport:
Footnote [17]:
This is described in ch. 67 of Padaratnāvalī’s text which is translated in the Appendix at ch. 59-III.

devanagari: ययाच आनम्य किरीटकोटिभिः । पादौ स्पृशन् अच्युतमर्थसाधनम् । सिद्धार्थ एतेन विगृह्यते महान् । अहो सुराणां च तमो धिगाढ्यताम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yayāca ānamya kirīṭakoṭibhiḥ pādau spṛśan acyutamarthasādhanam siddhārtha etena vigṛhyate mahān aho surāṇāṃ ca tamo dhigāḍhyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: Indra touched the feet of Kṛṣṇa with the crests of his crown many times while he bowed to him and begged of him to accomplish his purpose (viz. the recovery of Aditi’s earrings, Varuṇa’s royal umbrella and others). But once his purpose was achieved, he fought against him. What a great ignorance on the part of gods! Fie upon that affluence!

devanagari: अथो मुहूर्त एकस्मिन् नानागारेषु ताः स्त्रियः । यथोपयेमे भगवान् तावद् रूपधरोऽव्ययः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: atho muhūrta ekasmin nānāgāreṣu tāḥ striyaḥ yathopayeme bhagavān tāvad rūpadharo
translation:
Later on, the immutable Lord assumed as many forms as the number of damsels (brought from Naraka’s harem) and on the same specific auspicious time, he simultaneously yet severally married each one of them in their respective mansions with due formalities (and still remained changeless and perfect).
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:]
42(A). Lord Hari grants all the wishes of his devotees. He is infinite and incomprehensible. What is miraculous therein that he was severally and simultaneously present with each of his spouse?
42(B). Lord Kṛṣṇa who is the Supreme of all the masters of Yoga, is still visualized within, without and all around in hundreds and thousand forms by his devotees.

devanagari: गृहेषु तासामनपाय्यतर्ककृत् । निरस्तसाम्यातिशयेष्ववस्थितः । रेमे रमाभिर्निजकामसम्प्लुतो । यथेतरो गार्हकमेधिकांश्चरन् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛheṣu tāsāmanapāyyatarkakṛt nirastasāmyātiśayeṣvavasthitaḥ reme ramābhirnijakāmasampluto yathetaro gārhakamedhikāṃścaran

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: In their mansions which surpassed all comparisons and were endowed with super-abundant affluence, the imperishable Lord of incomprehensible activities remained present (all the while simultaneously). Though he was perfectly satisfied with his own bliss, he sported with those aṃśas (rays) of goddess Lakṣmī (viz. those newly wedded 16000 damsels) and like other house-holders attended to his house-hold duties.

devanagari: इत्थं रमापतिमवाप्य पतिं स्त्रियस्ता । ब्रह्मादयोऽपि न विदुः पदवीं यदीयाम् । भेजुर्मुदाविरतमेधितयानुराग । हासावलोकनवसङ्गमजल्पलज्जाः ॥ ४...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ ramāpatimavāpya patiṃ striyastā brahmādayo bhejurmudāviratamedhitayānurāga hāsāvalokanavasaṅgamajalpalajjāḥ
translation: Having obtained the consort of goddess Lakṣmī as their own spouse whose ways (or approaches to whom) are incomprehensible even to god Brahmā and others, those damsels continuously waited upon him with ever-increasing incessant delight, by means of their affectionate smiles, side-glances in their ever-new meetings and their bashful behaviour and speech.

devanagari: प्रत्युद्गमासनवरार्हणपदशौच । ताम्बूलविश्रमणवीजनगन्धमाल्यैः । केशप्रसारशयनस्नपनोपहार्यैः । दासीशता अपि विभोर्विदधुः स्म दास्यम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyudgamāsanavarārhaṇapadaśauca tāmbūlaviśramaṇavījanagandhamālyaiḥ keśaprasāraśayanasnapanopahāryaiḥ dāsīśatā api vibhorvidadhuḥ sma dāsyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ das ], [accusative single from √ das ]

translation:
Although hundreds of maid-servants were in their service, each of them personally rendered service to the Lord by rising up and advancing to receive him, offering him a seat and excellent articles of worship, washing his feet, offering betelleaves, removing his fatigue and refreshing him by fanning, application of sandalpaste and garlands of flowers, and by combing his hair, arranging his bed, bath and catering to him refreshments (or presents) to him.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । पारिजातहरणनरकवधो नाम एकोनषष्टितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe pārijātaharaṇanarakavadho nāma ekonaṣaṣṭitamo
